ban_set_location: Module-level global: _session_gap_stop_bans: set = set() — added alongside _market_cooldown in global state section

ban_registration_location.function: main() exit loop, inside gap_stop branch
ban_registration_location.context: elif reason == 'gap_stop' block — right after _market_cooldown update and 60m cooldown log
ban_registration_location.log_emitted: SESSION BAN [gap_stop] slug={slug} side={side} — same-side re-entry blocked for session

ban_check_location.context: Pre-gate check — before slug cap check (MAX_SLUG_ENTRIES_SESSION) and before check_entry_gates() call
ban_check_location.side_source: _intent_side = intent.get('side', '') — from the model bridge intent dict

opposite_side_behavior: Ban is keyed on (slug, side) tuple. A gap_stop on BUY_YES adds (slug, BUY_YES) to the set. BUY_NO entries on the same slug are NOT blocked — different tuple.
ban_scope: In-memory only — resets on restart. No DB or state.json changes. Intentional.

Confirmed trade.market_slug and trade.side available at that location
Added _session_gap_stop_bans global declaration to main() global statement
Confirmed pre-gate check uses intent['side'] not signal.side — correct source for bridge intents
restart_needed: Yes — ban set starts empty on restart (by design). All prior patches also require cold restart with pyc clear.
proven: Pre-gate check in bridge loop rejects entry when (market.slug, intent_side) is in ban set.
Ban is session-scoped (in-memory, resets on restart).
Log format matches BRIDGE GATE REJECT [check_entry_gates] pattern.
